description:
A HIPAA compliance audit has flagged the PATIENT_RECORDS table for storing Social Security Numbers and diagnosis codes in plaintext. Implement column-level encryption using the DBMS_CRYPTO package (AES-256). You must store a 256-bit key (KEY_ID=1) in a table named ENCRYPTION_KEY_STORE (KEY_ID, KEY_VALUE) and use PL/SQL functions ENCRYPT_VALUE and DECRYPT_VALUE to manage the data. Replace the plaintext SSN and DIAGNOSIS_CODE columns in PATIENT_RECORDS with encrypted RAW columns named SSN_ENCRYPTED and DIAGNOSIS_ENCRYPTED. Ensure the data is accessible through a view named PATIENT_RECORDS_VW that displays the decrypted information. Save a summary report of the implementation to /home/ga/Desktop/encryption_report.txt.

Database Connection: localhost:1521/XEPDB1, User: hr, Password: hr123
